items.final_wording: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

source.title: Atrial fibrillation: diagnosis and management
source.population: Adults with suspected, diagnosed, or previously treated atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter.
source.setting: Primary, outpatient, acute, and specialist cardiovascular care.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-led AF detection, risk and cardiac-function assessment, documented care review, and anticoagulation review. Treatment recommendations, named medicines, risk-score conclusions, and doses are not mapped, and UAE pathways require local adaptation.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1-1.1.3 - detection, 12-lead ECG, and ambulatory ECG

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

source.title: Atrial fibrillation: diagnosis and management
source.population: Adults with suspected, diagnosed, or previously treated atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter.
source.setting: Primary, outpatient, acute, and specialist cardiovascular care.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-led AF detection, risk and cardiac-function assessment, documented care review, and anticoagulation review. Treatment recommendations, named medicines, risk-score conclusions, and doses are not mapped, and UAE pathways require local adaptation.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1-1.1.3 - detection, 12-lead ECG, and ambulatory ECG

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

items.rationale: The NICE AF section supports documenting the ECG source and date, reason for review, related symptoms, pulse or rhythm context, and clinician interpretation where AF is suspected; it does not infer an ECG diagnosis.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

source.title: Recent-onset chest pain of suspected cardiac origin: assessment and diagnosis
source.population: Adults with recent-onset acute or stable chest pain or discomfort suspected to be cardiac in origin.
source.setting: Initial, urgent, primary, outpatient, and specialist assessment of recent-onset chest pain.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-documented chest-pain history, associated symptoms, risk context, examination and reviewed tests, information, and safety-net discussion. It is old and only partial for atypical or established non-acute follow-up; diagnosis, urgency, testing, treatment, and referral decisions are not generated.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1.1-1.1.1.9 - information, uncertainty, test discussion, and recurrent-pain advice

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.

items.rationale: The NICE chest-pain information section supports documenting clinician explanation of a reviewed test, uncertainty, questions, shared decisions, safety-netting, and follow-up without generating an interpretation or advice.

items.final_wording: Supports comprehensive clinician history and physical examination, ECG, directed blood testing, symptom frequency, selected ambulatory monitoring, and reversible or treatable cause context without generating a diagnosis or test order.

source.title: 2018 Guideline on the Evaluation and Management of Patients With Bradycardia and Cardiac Conduction Delay: Guidelines Made Simple
source.population: Patients with symptoms suggestive of or consistent with bradycardia or cardiac conduction disease.
source.setting: Initial, outpatient, acute, and specialist evaluation of suspected or documented bradycardia and conduction disorders.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-led history, physical examination, ECG and selected test review, symptom-rhythm correlation, reversible-cause context, and monitor selection. It is old, is a condensed official summary, and does not authorize automatic rhythm diagnosis, pacing, treatment, or referral.

exact_location.heading: Evaluation of Bradycardia and Conduction Disease Algorithm
exact_location.locator: Guidelines Made Simple PDF page 9, Figure 1, plus page 10 reversible-cause table

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports comprehensive clinician history and physical examination, ECG, directed blood testing, symptom frequency, selected ambulatory monitoring, and reversible or treatable cause context without generating a diagnosis or test order.

items.rationale: The bradycardia algorithm was reviewed to confirm ECG relevance for suspected bradycardia but was not generalized to every ECG result.

rewritten_legacy_items.final_wording: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.
